evals.name: start-learning-session
evals.prompt: I want to start learning from the comprehensive learning plan. Give me a task.
evals.expected_output: Should initialize progress, show summary with emojis (📊, 🔥, etc), and present first task (Task 1.1) with 📝 emoji, reference files, and instructions

assertions.name: shows_progress_summary
assertions.description: Output contains progress summary with 📊 emoji

assertions.name: presents_first_task
assertions.description: Presents Task 1.1 or similar first task

assertions.name: shows_reference_files
assertions.description: Shows reference file paths from learning plan

assertions.name: mentions_meta_learning
assertions.description: References meta-learning techniques (retrieval, spaced repetition, etc)

evals.name: continue-after-reading
evals.prompt: I've read the Program.cs file. I'm done with this task.
evals.expected_output: Should proceed to metacognitive confidence check (⏸️) and then quiz/verification with appropriate emojis

assertions.name: confidence_check
assertions.description: Shows confidence rating prompt with ⏸️ emoji

evals.name: report-struggle
evals.prompt: I'm struggling with understanding the middleware pipeline. It's confusing.
evals.expected_output: Should mark as struggle (❌), provide deeper explanation, offer elaboration questions (🔗), and acknowledge desirable difficulty

evals.name: ask-progress
evals.prompt: What's my learning progress so far?
evals.expected_output: Should show full progress summary (📊) with stats, mastery schedule (🔄), streak info (🔥), and scheduled reviews

assertions.name: mastery_schedule
assertions.description: Shows mastery or spaced repetition info

assertions.name: session_stats
assertions.description: Shows session count or total time

evals.name: request-elaboration
evals.prompt: Give me some elaboration questions about dependency injection.
evals.expected_output: Should provide 🔗 elaboration questions connecting DI to Django/previous knowledge, with 'why' and 'how does this relate' questions

assertions.name: explains_benefit
assertions.description: Explains benefit of elaboration for understanding

evals.name: teach-back-request
evals.prompt: I want to do a teach-back for LINQ queries to solidify my understanding.
evals.expected_output: Should provide 🎯 teach-back challenge asking user to explain LINQ in own words, cover key points, and mention teaching reveals gaps

assertions.name: explain_in_own_words
assertions.description: Asks user to explain in own words

assertions.name: teaching_benefits
assertions.description: Mentions that teaching reveals gaps or solidifies understanding

evals.name: quiz-answer-correct
evals.prompt: My answer to the middleware quiz: The correct order is UseExceptionHandler → UseAuthentication → UseAuthorization because each middleware can short-circuit the pipeline, and we want to catch auth errors gracefully.
evals.expected_output: Should mark as passed (✅), update mastery, acknowledge correct reasoning, and potentially show celebration for correct after struggle

assertions.name: mastery_updated
assertions.description: Mentions mastery schedule update or next review

assertions.name: correct_feedback
assertions.description: Provides positive feedback for correct answer

evals.name: confidence-rating-before-quiz
evals.prompt: I rate my confidence as 4 - I understand well and could explain middleware pipeline to someone else.
evals.expected_output: Should save rating, proceed to quiz, and warn about illusion of competence if rating is high (≥4)

assertions.name: illusion_warning
assertions.description: Shows ⚠️ warning about illusion of competence for high ratings

evals.name: request-interleaved-session
evals.prompt: I want an interleaved session mixing different topics.
evals.expected_output: Should show 🎲 interleaved session with mix of current + previous + random topics, explain interleaving benefits

assertions.name: current_plus_review
assertions.description: Includes both current task and review/previous topic

evals.name: check-spaced-repetition
evals.expected_output: Should show 🔄 mastery schedule with tasks, intervals, next review dates, and strength levels
